summary: Retest of iteration_12's 3 refund 500s + password_hash export redaction. RESULT: 23/23 backend tests pass — the previously-failing POST /api/refund-requests, /refund-requests/{id}/deny, and adjacent audit paths now return 200 (domain.write_audit short-circuits when org_id is None). /api/auth/me/export returns 200 with a valid ZIP whose account/profile.json has NEITHER `password_hash` NOR `passwordHash` keys. Regression on tier gating, checkout, portal, webhook, /payments/me all still pass (17/17 in test_phase2_billing.py). Extra: /refund-requests/{id}/approve with a fake UUID returns 404 (not 500). Frontend spot check: /pricing renders 3 plan cards (Opportunity Intelligence $49/mo, Full Capture & Proposal Generation $99/mo with RECOMMENDED pill, Enterprise Contact us) with no errors; /admin → Refunds tab loads for QA (platform_owner) and renders the empty-state ('No refund requests · Nothing in the pending queue.') without any crash after the pending REGRESSION_ row was denied at teardown.
action_items: OPTIONAL (carried from iter 10/11/12): silence React Router v6→v7 future-flag warnings by adding v7_startTransition + v7_relativeSplatPath to BrowserRouter — cosmetic, non-blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/domain.py write_audit: now correctly no-ops when org_id is None, which unblocks the billing.refund_* audit calls. Note: for future auditability, consider inserting these rows into a separate 'platform_audit_log' table (org_id nullable) or under a synthetic 'billing' org sentinel so refund approvals/denials still leave a durable audit trail. Not blocking Phase 2 launch — the app-level logger already records the actor + action, and refund_requests rows carry decidedBy/decidedAt.

backend/routers/auth.py /me/export: profile.json now pops both `password_hash` and `passwordHash`. Verified via pytest that neither key appears in the ZIP. Good defensive fix — covers both the legacy DB column snake_case and any camelCase alias produced by the serializer.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter13_regression.py (new file — 6 pytest cases explicitly covering the iter_12 regressions: refund create/deny 200, decidedAt populated, approve-with-fake-id → 404, and password_hash/passwordHash absent from export ZIP)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (/pricing 3-plan render + /admin Refunds tab empty-state render both verified via Playwright on the preview URL)

seed_data_creation: 1 refund_requests row inserted for QA with reason='REGRESSION_test1' during test_submit_refund_regression; row was immediately denied by test_deny_no_more_500 and swept a second time by the module-scoped teardown fixture. Also, test_phase2_billing.py::TestRefunds inserted 1 row with reason='TEST_refund by pytest' which was successfully denied by test_deny_refund_and_appears_in_denied. Both rows now sit in refund_requests.status='denied' — safe to leave (they will not resurface in the platform-owner Pending queue), or main agent can DELETE FROM refund_requests WHERE reason LIKE 'REGRESSION_%' OR reason LIKE 'TEST_%' if a cleaner slate is desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 billing/tier-gating/refunds/export is fully green on the preview URL (https://govcon-workspace.preview.emergentagent.com). Two pytest files cover the acceptance criteria: /app/backend/tests/test_phase2_billing.py (17 cases) and /app/backend/tests/test_iter13_regression.py (6 cases). QA account (qa.captureagent@testmail.dev / CaptureQA#2026) is untouched, tier=free, isPlatformOwner=true. Do NOT hit /api/refund-requests/{id}/approve with a real request id — QA has no real Stripe payment_intent; the fake-uuid → 404 path is already covered. frontend/.env still points at the preview URL. Before deploying to prod, flip REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL back to https://captureagent.us and restart frontend.
rca_of_the_issue: Iteration_12 root cause was: domain.write_audit performed an INSERT into audit_log (organization_id NOT NULL), but routers/payments.py refund endpoints called write_audit(None, user, ...). The refund_requests row committed first, then the audit INSERT raised asyncpg.NotNullViolationError → HTTP 500. Fix (verified in this iteration): write_audit now short-circuits and returns immediately when org_id is None. Verified by (a) pytest test_submit_refund_request → 200 ok:true with a requestId, (b) test_deny_refund_and_appears_in_denied → 200 ok:true with decidedAt populated in the denied list, (c) approve with a bogus UUID → 404 (not 500), and (d) via frontend Playwright: /admin Refunds tab loads and renders without a JS crash. Secondary fix in auth.py /me/export (pop both `password_hash` and `passwordHash` from profile.json) is also verified — pytest opens the ZIP and asserts both keys are absent.
